display-lint — closing the bypass (found by independent review, hours after ship)

display-lint (#2930, shipped v8.77.0) was reviewed adversarially by a code-quality-reviewer agent that had never seen the implementation. It found two logic bugs; both were reproduced by hand against the live built dispatcher before being accepted as real, then fixed.

Bug 1 — the hook could be trivially defeated

BEFORE: SCRIPT_INVOCATION_RE.test(command) — matches ANYWHERE in the string → append " && bash /tmp/anything.sh" (file need not exist) to ANY cluttered command and the entire lint goes silent AFTER: stages.length === 1 && SCRIPT_INVOCATION_RE.test(stages[0]) → exemption only applies when the ENTIRE command is the script call

Verified live before the fix: baseline command → deny. Same command + && bash /tmp/noop.sh → silently passed. After the fix: denied, as intended.

Bug 2 — heredoc exemption caught unrelated syntax

BEFORE: command.includes('<<') — bare substring match → a bash bit-shift like `console.log(1 << 4)` falsely exempted an otherwise-cluttered command AFTER: HEREDOC_RE requires a real delimiter right after << (letter, underscore, or quote — never a digit) so `<< 4` / `<<$n` never match, only `<<EOF` / `<<'EOF'` / `<<-EOF` do

Verification

CheckResult
13 unit tests (5 new: the 2 bypass regressions + 2 boundary tests + 1 multiline case)PASS
Full suite291 files / 7192 tests PASS
Typecheckclean
Biomeclean (0 new; 3 pre-existing config warnings unrelated)
Both bugs reproduced by hand against the live built dispatcherconfirmed before AND after the fix

This is the fast-follow from an /ork:verify run of the session's shipped work — display-lint itself was correctly positioned in the dispatcher chain (no security regression from insertion), the bug was isolated to its own exemption logic.
